channel.title: Health on VitaminDWiki
channel.description: Recent content in Health on VitaminDWiki

item.title: Prescription drugs are the 3rd cause of all US deaths, 4th cause of deaths in hospital
item.description:
Prescription Drugs Are the Leading Cause of Death

Brownstone

  • “37,309 drug deaths were reported to the FDA in 2006 and
    123,927 ten years later, which is 3.3 times as many”

  • “Depression drugs kill many people, mainly among the elderly, because they can cause orthostatic hypotension, sedation, confusion, and dizziness. The drugs double the risk of falls and hip fractures in a dose-dependent manner,8,9 and within one year after a hip fracture, about one-fifth of the patients will have died. As elderly people often fall anyway, it is not possible to know if such deaths are drug deaths.”

item.title: Early-Onset diseases and Vitamin D - several studies
item.description:
Candidate causes include:
Low vitamin D (which can be fought by Vitamin D)
((Microplastics now causing problems in most life forms - many studies Microplastics))
((Ultra-processed foods associated with worse health and lower Vitamin D - several studies Ultra-processed foods))
((Air Pollution reduces Vitamin D Air pollution))
((DDT and other pesticides decrease vitamin D Pesticides))
      • Why are so many young people getting cancer? - April 2024
      • 40,700 studies had EARLY-ONSET in their title as of April 2024

Why are so many young people getting cancer? - April 2024

VOX

item.title: Ultra-processed foods associated with worse health and lower Vitamin D - several studies
item.description:
• Ultra-processed food and adverse health outcomes – BMJ meta-analysis Jan 2024
      • The Perils of Ultra-processed foods (article and book) - July 2023
      • VitaminDWiki - Ultra-processed food consumption: 2X increased risk of vitamin D deficiency – Dec 2023
      • Ultra-processed foods increase the risk of metabolic syndromes - Jan 2024
      • Increased Ultra-processed foods increase risks of Cancer and CVD by 9% - Lancet Dec 2023
      • Ultra-processed foods can be as addictive as cocaine or smoking - Feb 2024
      • Perplexity AI on ultra-processed foods - March 2024

Ultra-processed food and adverse health outcomes – BMJ meta-analysis Jan 2024

Credible increased risk of 9 health problems (a subset of table)

item.title: Which health problems take more than 10 years to develop - Chat-GPT
item.description:
• Chat-GPT answer
      • VitaminDWiki - Maternal vitamin D deficiency can result in problems 1 or 2 decades later - May 2023

Chat-GPT answer

Several health conditions can take more than 10 years to develop, often due to a combination of genetic, lifestyle, and environmental factors. Here’s a list of some notable conditions that typically have a long development period:

  1. Cardiovascular Diseases : Heart disease, including atherosclerosis (hardening and narrowing of the arteries), can develop over many years. Lifestyle factors like diet, exercise, and smoking play a significant role in their progression.

item.title: 16 Longevity books compared in a video
item.description:
• Peter Attia’s Longevity Book Outlive: The BEST or WORST longevity book?
      • VitaminDWiki - Longevity and healthspan increased by Vitamin D - many studies
      • VitaminDWiki - How Not To Age book (diet, exercise, supplements) Dr. Greger - Dec 2023

Peter Attia’s Longevity Book Outlive: The BEST or WORST longevity book?

YouTube 35 minutes

Books

  1. Outlive: The Science and Art of Longevity by Peter Attia

  2. The Longevity Project: Surprising Discoveries for Health and Long Life from the Landmark Eight-Decade Study

item.title: Chronic Health Conditions: 27% of Americans have more than 1 of the top 10 - CDC 2018
item.description:
Prevalence of Multiple Chronic Conditions Among US Adults, 2018

CDC Sept 2020

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Top 10 chronic conditions(before COVID and Long-COVID)

  1. arthritis,

  2. cancer,

  3. chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD),

  4. coronary heart disease,

  5. current asthma,

  6. diabetes,

  7. hepatitis,

  8. hypertension,

  9. stroke, and

  10. weak or failing kidney

#00F:All of the above are prevented/treated by Vitamin D

VitaminDWiki - Many Chronic Health Diseases and Infections can controlled by 40 ng of Vitamin D – Aug 2023

item.title: Stem cells and Vitamin D - many studies
Down syndrome people have even lower D levels if have additional Vitamin D diseases
item.description:
• Hypovitaminosis D in persons with Down syndrome and autism spectrum disorder
      • VitaminDWiki - Down syndrome and low vitamin D - several studies

Hypovitaminosis D in persons with Down syndrome and autism spectrum disorder

J Neurodev Disord . 2023 Oct 25;15(1):35. doi: 10.1186/s11689-023-09503-y.

Natalie K Boyd 1, Julia Nguyen 2, Mellad M Khoshnood 1, Timothy Jiang 1, Lina Nguyen 1, Lorena Mendez 1, Noemi A Spinazzi 3, Melanie A Manning 4, Michael S Rafii 5 6, Jonathan D Santoro 7 8

item.title: Doctors are incentivised to prescribe medicines, not improve health
item.description:
• How PCPs are penalized for positive outcomes from lifestyle change

How PCPs are penalized for positive outcomes from lifestyle change

MD Edge

One example: Dr. reduced diabetes by lifestyle changes

  • However, the CMS 5-Star Rating System assigned the primary care physician a grade of C rather than A, which put the physician›s 5-star rating at risk. Why? Because the system bases its score largely on medication compliance . The physician was penalized despite achieving the optimal health outcome, and at a lower cost than with medication. "

Medicare incentivizes disease management, not disease reversal

item.title: Live 24 years longer if have 8 habits (survey of 700,000 veterans)
item.description:
• The eight habits researchers recommend to lengthen life expectancy
      • What habits will extend life expectancy?
      • See also: Longevity and healthspan increased by Vitamin D - many studies 80+ studies
      • See also: Ways to improve health

The eight habits researchers recommend to lengthen life expectancy

URL

According to the results, men who practised all eight habits by age 40 would be predicted to live an average of 24 years longer than men with none of these habits.
For women, having all eight healthy lifestyle factors in middle age was associated with a predicted 21 additional years of life compared to women with none of these habits.

item.title: US Healthcare spending to consume 20% of GDP by 2028
item.description:
• Modern HealthCare March 2022
      • It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

Modern HealthCare March 2022

It appears that Vitamin D can cut the cost of healthcare in half

Hope to have a demonstration with 5,000 people in 2024 getting 50,0000 IU weekly

item.title: Rare diseases: 1 in 10 Americans (some are helped by Vitamin D) - 2023
item.description:
There are more than 10,000 known rare diseases.

Approximately 80% of rare diseases are estimated to have a genetic origin.

Of those, 70% start in childhood. (56% of rare diseases start in childhood)

  • “In the United States, about half of the people with a rare disease are children. Many of these diseases are chronic and worsen over time.”

The US defines incidence of a “rare disease” as being 1 in every 1,500 people

item.title: The top 10 medical expenses can all be decreased by vitamin D
item.description:
• The health problems & expenses WebMD Nov 2021

The health problems & expenses WebMD Nov 2021
Problem Cost VitaminDWiki
Heart Conditions: $555 Billion Overview Cardiovascular and vitamin D
Diabetes: $245 Billion Overview Diabetes and vitamin D
Cancer: $225 Billion Overview Cancer and vitamin D and 17+ subcategories
Obesity: $190 Billion Overview Obesity and Vitamin D
COPD and Asthma: Between $100-290 Billion COPD fought by Vitamin D - many studies
Overview Asthma and Vitamin D
Traffic Injuries: $99 Billion ((Vitamin D is needed before most surgeries – many studies and RCTs
Mental Disorders: $89 Billion Overview Alzheimer’s-Cognition and Vitamin D
Depression
Brain and Vitamin D - many studies
Low Back and Neck Problems: $87 Billion Back Pain
Joint Disorders: $80 Billion Overview Osteoporosis and vitamin D
High Blood Pressure: $46 Billion Overview Hypertension and Vitamin D

Heart Conditions, Diabetes, Cancer, Obesity, COPD and Asthma, Traffic Injuries, Mental Disorders, Depression; Brain, Low Back and Neck Problems, Joint Disorders, High Blood Pressure,

item.title: Off-topic: Depression gone after participating in 5 day Tony Robins event
item.description:
• The Exciting Emergence of Regenerative Medicine - Mercola May 2022
      • 2022 Book: Life Force by Robins - with Table of Contents
      • 2022 video of T. Robbins, P. Diamandis and R. Patrick on Life extension

The Exciting Emergence of Regenerative Medicine - Mercola May 2022

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

  • “A Stanford clinical research study of Robbins’ “Date With Destiny” program found 100% of participants with clinical depression were symptom-free 30 days after completion of the five-and-a-half-day course”

item.title: Poor odor detection if low Vitamin D, better after 50,000 IU weekly
item.description:
• Effect of vitamin D on olfactory function
      • This study concerns the ability to detect a smell, NOT the loss of smell
      • Loss of smell is associated with COVID, Long-Haul, andl Low Zinc
      • VitaminDWiki - Perhaps drugs such as inhaled vitamin D can get directly to the brain – March 2018

Effect of vitamin D on olfactory function

B-ENT 2021; 17(2): 86-91 ALLERGY AND RHINOLOGY DOI: 10.5152/B-ENT.2021.21041

Ahmet Baki1 © Muhammet Yildiz2 G, Sel$uk Gundogar3 ©

item.title: Vitamin D is important to health
item.description:
Non-Musculoskeletal Benefits of Vitamin D beyond the Musculoskeletal System

Int. J. Mol. Sci. 2021, 22(4), 2128; https://doi.org/10.3390/ijms22042128

Sicheng Zhang, Duane D. Miller andWei Li wli@uthsc.edu

Department of Pharmaceutical Sciences, College of Pharmacy, The University of Tennessee Health Science Center, Memphis, TN 38163, USA

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

Table of Contents

Calcium and Phosphorus

Metabolism

Immune System

Vitamin D, a fat-soluble prohormone, is endogenously synthesized in response to sunlight or taken from dietary supplements. Since vitamin D receptors are present in most tissues and cells in the body, the mounting understanding of the role of vitamin D in humans indicates that it does not only play an important role in the musculoskeletal system, but has beneficial effects elsewhere as well.

item.title: Live 7 years longer free of major disease if eat well, not smoke, not obese, moderate drink and exercise (100,000 people)
item.description:
Healthy lifestyle and life expectancy free of cancer, cardiovascular disease, and type 2 diabetes: prospective cohort study

BMJ 2020; 368 doi: https://doi.org/10.1136/bmj.l6669

7 years longer without Cancer, CVD, Diabetes if follow 4 of the following 5 lifestyle factors

Vitamin D is the 3rd most important contributor to health,
    and the lowest-cost way to improve health

 

This study does not mention Vitamin D, which is not as effctive but is far lower cost and easier

item.title: Millennials (1981-1999) are sicker and dying faster than previous generations (Vitamin D can help)
item.description:
• Millennials Will Get Sick and Die Faster Than the Previous Generation
      • Fewer Accidents with Vitamin D

Download the Blue Cross Blue Shield PDF from VitaminDWiki

This study will tend to not notice health problems of those who do not have Health Insurance

item.title: Patients believe that doctors do not properly recognize and treat 7 of the top 10 chronic illnesses
item.description:
No Future for Medicine Dr. Dean

  1. Asthma

  2. Obesity

  3. Osteoarthritis

  4. Hearing loss

  5. Eczema

  6. Insomnia

  7. Anxiety

OK FOR

  1. Hypothyroidism

  2. Diabetes

  3. Hypertension

Article shows how many of the problems are treated by Magnesium

VitaminDWiki also has many studies on how each problem is treated by Vitamin D

    See the left hand column of any page in VitaminDWiki

Reference was a survey

Patient-Physician Agreement in Reporting and Prioritizing Existing Chronic Conditions Sept 2019

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

item.title: Muscular Dystrophy probably treated by high-dose Vitamin D plus muscle rehab
item.description:
Muscular Dystrophies – low vitamin D might be associated with all 9 types – Aug 2018

      • Muscular Dystrophy treated by high-dose Coimbra Protocol (Facebook Aug 2019)
      • Fracture in Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy: Natural History and Vitamin D Deficiency – 2016
      • People with DMD need more Vitamin D - meta-analysis Oct 2023
      • Need 3X more vitamin D if DMD - 2019
      • Limb-girdle muscular dystrophy - perhaps caused by a poor gene - a treatment in 2023
      • Muscular Dystrophy and genes
      • People with DMD have poor response to Vitamin D - 2017 thesis
      • Mechanisms in Sarcopenia and Muscular Dystrophy - Aug 2015
      • VitaminDWiki - Multiple Sclerosis and Vitamin D ~ 50,000 Facebook members in 13 languages – Jan 2017
      • Life Extension Magazine Overview of Muscular Dystrophy - Dec 2020
      • 95% of MD patients had low Vitamin D - 2019
      • 10X increase in DMD in 16 years - 2020
      • Vitamin K2: A Cure for Muscular Dystrophy?- Video July 2022
      • Overview of muscular dystrophies (Glucocorticoids decrease Vitamin D getting to cells) - Sept 2023
      • New $$$ Duchenne MD drug - fewer side effects than Corticosteroids - Oct 2023
      • FDA approved Duvyzat (it reduced the decline of speed of MD children to climb stairs over 18 months) - March 2024

Muscular Dystrophy treated by high-dose Coimbra Protocol (Facebook Aug 2019)

Facebooktranslated

item.title: Vitamin D appears to fight Diabetes, MS, RA, Lupus, IBD, Hepatitis, Cancer, Psoriasis, Food allergy, etc
Health Care is a Human Right in a most countries, but not the US
item.description:
What It Means for Health Care to Be a Human Right The Atlantic June 2019

  • “ More than half the world’s countries have pledged to protect their citizens’ right to health care, either through national laws or international human-rights agreements ”

  • Only 8 percent of US adults get all high-priority Screening (even fewer get Prevention) – June 2018

item.title: 35 benefits of Vitamin D – Self-Hacked
item.description:
Self-Hacked

By Maja Stanojevic, MD
Has lots of references with hyperlinks
These and many more are in the left-hand column of this page
The following are just the titles.

    1. Crucial to Bone Health
    1. Prevents Rickets and Osteomalacia
    1. Prevents Osteoporosis and Fractures
    1. Can Improve Physical Performance
    1. May Protect Against Cancer
    1. Beneficial for Brain Development and Function
    1. Improves Cognitive Functions
    1. Reduces Depression
    1. Reduces the Risk of Parkinson’s Disease
    1. Plays a Role in Alzheimer’s Disease
    1. Beneficial in Multiple Sclerosis
    1. Improves Sleep Quality
    1. Reduces the Risk of Cardiovascular Disease
    1. Reduces Blood Pressure
    1. May Decrease the Risk of Type 1 and Type 2 Diabetes
    1. Prevents Obesity and Metabolic Syndrome
    1. Anti-Inflammatory
    1. May Be Beneficial For Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Celiac Disease
    1. Plays a Role in Cystic Fibrosis
    1. Beneficial in Atopic Dermatitis
    1. Effective Therapy for Psoriasis Patients
    1. May Benefit Thyroid Dysfunction
    1. Reduces the Severity of Asthma
    1. Affects Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
    1. Reduces Risk of Tuberculosis
    1. Has a Negative Effect on Sarcoidosis
    1. Decreases the Risk of Respiratory Tract Infections
    1. Plays a Role in Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
    1. Beneficial in Rheumatoid Arthritis
    1. Protects Kidney Function
    1. Beneficial in HIV Disease
    1. Affects Male Fertility
    1. Beneficial in Pregnancy and Lactation
    1. Improves Clinical Manifestations of Polycystic Ovary Syndrome
    1. Influences Hair Loss

item.title: Double digit increases in many health problems for those aged 21-36 in just 4 years
item.description:
The Health of Millennials BlueCross, BlueShield]

This report focuses on the 55 million millennial Americans that are commercially insured”

  • “The BCBS Health Index assigns U.S. populations a score between 0 and 100. A score of ‘100’ represents optimal health—a population’s potential lifespan, absent of disability and increased risk of death. The higher the score, the closer a population is to achieving optimal health. The lower the score, the more a population has been adversely impacted by certain health conditions.”

item.title: Cancer stem cells and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
• Repurposing vitamin D for treatment of human malignancies via targeting tumor microenvironment – March 2019
      • Mechanisms of anticancer action of vitamin D within tumor microenvironment
      • 3.2 Cancer stem cells
      • References
    • Role of vitamin D in targeting cancer and cancer stem cell populations and its therapeutic implications - Oct 2022
    • A Guide to Cancer Stem Cell Markers (Gastric, Bladder. Brain, Liver, etc) - March 2022
    • Vitamin D Compounds and Cancer Stem Cells in Cancer Prevention - April 2020
    • Cancer stem cells revisited - Oct 2017
    • Stem Cells - What are they and why are they important (to Multiple Sclerosis) 2019?
    • Vitamin D can be used as a supplement_against cancer stem cells - Sept 2018
    • Boosting the effects of vitamin D to tackle diabetes - May 2018 Stem Cells Portal
    • How Vitamin D Stops Cancer Stem Cells - 2016?
    • See also VitaminDWiki

Repurposing vitamin D for treatment of human malignancies via targeting tumor microenvironment – March 2019

Acta Pharmaceutica Sinica B, Vol 9, Issue 2, March 2019, Pages 203-219. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.apsb.2018.09.002

item.title: Searching for Vitamin D studies – AllMEDx is a superset of PubMed
item.description:
https://allmedx.com/

      • Sources of information
      • Can subsort a search on
      • Also includes subtopics
      • Does not include non-human, preclinical studies, nor studies >10 years old
      • Unfortunately puts …… in long titles, making them hard to understand
      • Unable to search for word in title
      • Supported by advertising

Sources of information

  1. Centers for Disease Control (CDC): thousands of articles and alerts from the nation’s health protection agency

  2. Cleveland Clinic: free full text of hundreds of Patient Education handouts

item.title: Ways to improve health
item.description:
My opinion - which is based on reading >20,000 related studies

I would love to see attempts at quantifying this

    Henry Lahore, founder of VitaminDWiki,   2019

      • “A 2013 report on the state of US health identified dietary factors as the single most significant risk factor for disability and premature death”
      • Half of Diabetics cured in 16 days by eating a plant-based diet – Dr. Greger Sept 2019
      • Live 14 years longer with healthy lifestyle (2 years longer if good vitamin D) – Dr. Greger Oct 2019
      • Cost effective for US Medicare/Medicaid to give a 30% discount on healthy food - March 2019
      • Live Longer - data-based 2019
      • Yet again, review of poor nutrition education for doctors - JAMA March 2019
      • Exercise
      • Ultraprocessed foods - an excellent article describing a book - July 2023

“A 2013 report on the state of US health identified dietary factors as the single most significant risk factor for disability and premature death”

Download the PDF from VitaminDWiki

item.title: Increased incidence of health problems if iow Vitamin D
item.description:
4X higher risk

Age-Related Macular Degeneration - wet

Cancer - advanced-stage

Childhood asthma

Cognitive Impairment

Diabetes T1

Frail when old

Increased risk of death if n ICU for 2 days

Lung disease - non-TB

Metabolic syndrome

Multiple sclerosis

Pneumonia acquired in hospital ==> death

Pregnancy - C-section, Preeclampsia

Psychiatric disorders as a teen

5X more likely

Allergic rhinitis

Childhood Cancer

Coronary Artery Disease

Coronary bypass ==> death

Hip Fracture ==> bad outcome

item.title: Far healthier and stronger at age 72 due to supplements
item.description:
Personal observation by the founder of VitaminDWiki

I noticed my health improve with each added supplement

Vitamin D for 8+ years (~100 ng)

Good intake of Magnesium and Vitamin K for 6 years

Vectomega brand of Omega-3 for 4+ years (tested)- previously had taken Coromega brand for a decade

        While my health had improved a lot, I felt that I was not getting the full vitamin D benefit

A poor Vitamin D Receptor will limit the benefits of Vitamin D

item.title: Healthspan is more important than Lifespan – JAMA
item.description:
• From Lifespan to Healthspan - 2018
      • Might be able to “square the curve” with Vitamin D
      • 193 studies cited this study as of Dec 2023

From Lifespan to Healthspan - 2018

JAMA. 2018;320(13):1323-1324. doi:10.1001/jama.2018.12621

S. Jay Olshansky, PhD1

#### Chart from PDF

1900

  • Many deaths before age 3

  • Adult death rates inacreas at age 20, short lifetimes, peak at age 71

2016

  • Adult death rates increase at age 50, long lifetimes, peak at age 90

item.title: What is the easiest way to get healthy: Vitamin D, Healthy Food, or Exercise
item.description:
• Let us assume, for argument, that they result in similar health benefits
      • Ways to improve health has a venn diagram of food, exercise and supplements
      • VitaminDWiki - Ultra-processed food consumption: 2X increased risk of vitamin D deficiency – Dec 2023

Let us assume, for argument, that they result in similar health benefits

Now let us estimate the monthly costs and time for each.

Does include: purchase, preparation, use, and cleanup

Does not include travel (time or cost)

item.title: Peroxynitrite – possible cause of increases in many health problems
item.description:
• The Root Cause in the dramatic rise of Chronic Disease – draft Jan 2018
      • Growth of Chronic Disease
      • Observations by VitaminDWiki on Peroxynitrite and Vitamin D
      • Increases in 25 years
      • Huge increases in some diseases: peroxynitrite, Vitamin D, toxins, or what?

The Root Cause in the dramatic rise of Chronic Disease – draft Jan 2018

First page of PDF

NOTE to Readers: I am seeking one or two scientists to take the following research and paper forward. There are really two papers here. One is a paper that strengthens the scientific connection between peroxynitrite and at least forty fast-growing chronic diseases. The second paper is a translation of the science that can be offered to the American people, who remain unaware of the root cause of the dramatic increase of chronic disease and conditions in the US. More than 170 million Americans are currently suffering from diseases and conditions that can be vastly improved or even reversed by reducing levels of peroxynitrite through moderate changes in lifestyle, the immediate environment and diet plus nonprescription supplementation. While scientists recognize the pivotal role of peroxynitrite in disease, few policy makers and physicians are aware of the opportunity they have to heal a nation suffering from chronic diseases. The annual cost of just forty fast-growing diseases is more than $2.5 trillion. With increased awareness and action this cost to society can be reduced to a fraction by simply implementing the knowledge we already have.
